Subjective:  
58-year-old woman with 3-week history of worsening exertional dyspnea and intermittent palpitations. Denies chest pain, syncope, orthopnea, or PND. Reports good medication adherence and no new drug changes.

Objective:  
Vitals: BP 148/92, HR 102 irregular, SpO2 96% RA, BMI 31. Exam shows irregularly irregular rhythm, clear lungs, trace pretibial edema. ECG: atrial fibrillation, ventricular rate 98, no ischemic changes. BNP: 312 pg/mL.

Assessment:  
New-onset atrial fibrillation with rate control issue and elevated BNP, likely heart failure with preserved ejection fraction. Hype
